# Install Test Fixtures

This directory backs `tests/unit/install.bats` with a hermetic, network-free
install path. `install.sh` is pointed at a local HTTP server (started by
the bats `setup_file`) via the `BASE_URL` env var, and the server serves
files from this directory.

## Layout

| File / pattern                      | Source-controlled | Notes                                                       |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `VERSION`                           | yes               | Pinned fixture version, read by `pack.sh` and `install.bats` |
| `pack.sh`                           | yes               | Generates the tarball + `SHA256SUMS` from `dist/openspec-extended` |
| `README.md`                         | yes               | This file                                                   |
| `releases/` (everything inside)     | no (gitignored)   | Generated by `pack.sh`                                      |

## Regenerating fixtures

The bats `setup_file` calls `pack.sh` automatically before starting the
HTTP server, so you usually don't need to run it manually. To regenerate
by hand:

```bash
bash tests/fixtures/install/pack.sh                          # version from VERSION file, linux-x86_64
bash tests/fixtures/install/pack.sh 0.19.0 darwin-arm64      # explicit version + platform
```

`pack.sh` writes `openspec-extended-v{VERSION}-{PLATFORM}.tar.gz` and a
matching `SHA256SUMS` file. The tarball layout mirrors what the
`.github/workflows/release.yml` matrix job produces:

```
openspec-extended/
└── bin/
    └── openspec-extended
```

The fixture is built from `dist/openspec-extended`, so run `mise run build`
first if you don't already have a binary. `mise run verify` does this
automatically.
